metadata.dispatch_line: Possible domestic disturbance at 1453 Oakwood Drive, apartment 3B, reported by a neighbor, Springfield, Illinois

scenario.dispatch_line: Possible domestic disturbance at 1453 Oakwood Drive, apartment 3B, reported by a neighbor, Springfield, Illinois

facts.domestic_violence_04: Yes, the officer provided assistance and support to the victim, Ms. Leila Patel, at 1453 Oakwood Drive, apartment 3B, Springfield, Illinois
facts.domestic_violence_05: Priority response assigned due to intimate partner violence call
facts.domestic_violence_06: Yes, information about obtaining an order of protection was provided to Ms. Patel
facts.domestic_violence_07: Yes, the time of the call is recorded: incident occurred at 21:45, dispatch at 21:50, and arrival at 22:00 on February 10, 2023
facts.domestic_violence_08: Yes, information about local service providers, including the Springfield Victim Assistance Program, was provided to Ms. Patel
facts.domestic_violence_09: Yes, the elements of the crime(s) articulated meet state and/or federal laws that address domestic violence
facts.domestic_violence_10: Yes, a threat/risk assessment tool was used with Ms. Patel
facts.domestic_violence_11: Upon approach, the officer observed a damaged front door, broken glass, and signs of a struggle at the scene
facts.domestic_violence_12: Yes, a valid protection order is in place, issued on January 20, 2023, prohibiting Mr. Ryan Jenkins from contacting or approaching Ms. Patel
facts.domestic_violence_13: Yes, the scene is concisely described and diagramed in the report, including the location of 1453 Oakwood Drive, apartment 3B, Springfield, Illinois
facts.domestic_violence_14: Yes, photos were taken and details recorded, including the damaged property and injuries sustained by Ms. Patel
facts.domestic_violence_15: The relationship of the parties is identified as intimate partners, with Ms. Patel and Mr. Jenkins having been in a romantic relationship for 3 years
facts.domestic_violence_16: The history of the relationship includes frequent incidents of verbal and physical abuse, with Ms. Patel reporting 5 previous incidents in the past year
facts.domestic_violence_17: Yes, all witnesses, including Ms. Patel's neighbor, Mr. Kaito Nakamura, were interviewed and documented
facts.domestic_violence_18: Yes, an arrest was made, with Mr. Jenkins being taken into custody at 23:15 on February 10, 2023
facts.domestic_violence_19: No, there were no children on the scene
facts.domestic_violence_20: Yes, information about previous incidents was documented, including the 5 previous incidents reported by Ms. Patel
facts.domestic_violence_21: Yes, a weapon/object was used, with Mr. Jenkins allegedly using a kitchen knife to threaten Ms. Patel
facts.domestic_violence_22: The emotional state of the victim, Ms. Patel, was distressed and fearful, with visible signs of anxiety and trauma
facts.domestic_violence_24: Photos of injuries, witness statements, and physical evidence such as the kitchen knife
facts.domestic_violence_25: No, the threat/risk assessment tool was only used with Ms. Patel
facts.domestic_violence_26: Yes, Ms. Patel's fearful state is documented in the report
facts.domestic_violence_27: Yes, all threats made by Mr. Jenkins, including the threat to harm Ms. Patel with the kitchen knife, are documented
facts.domestic_violence_28: Not applicable, as the suspect was present on the scene
facts.domestic_violence_29: Yes, the use of coercion and force by Mr. Jenkins is articulated in the report
facts.domestic_violence_30: Yes, all injuries, including Ms. Patel's bruised arm and scratched face, are documented
facts.domestic_violence_31: Both, Ms. Patel had existing injuries from previous incidents and new injuries from the current incident
facts.domestic_violence_32: Yes, there was property damage, including the broken front door and glass
facts.domestic_violence_33: Yes, stalking behaviors such as constant phone calls and showing up at Ms. Patel's workplace are identified
facts.domestic_violence_34: Yes, the officer inquired about possible strangulation and Ms. Patel reported difficulty breathing and a raspy voice
facts.domestic_violence_35: Yes, Ms. Patel reported being strangled by Mr. Jenkins during the incident
facts.domestic_violence_36: Yes, Ms. Patel requested medical attention and was transported to the hospital by EMS
facts.domestic_violence_37: No, Ms. Patel did not report sexual violence during the incident
facts.domestic_violence_38: Yes, all spontaneous statements, including Ms. Patel's statement that Mr. Jenkins had been threatening her, are captured
facts.domestic_violence_39: Yes, the officer asked about firearms and Ms. Patel reported that Mr. Jenkins has a gun at his residence, 2345 Maple Street, Springfield, Illinois
facts.domestic_violence_40: No, the suspect did not use firearms during the incident
facts.domestic_violence_41: Yes, Mr. Jenkins made threats to use firearms against Ms. Patel in the past
facts.domestic_violence_42: Yes, the suspect has access to firearms, as reported by Ms. Patel
facts.domestic_violence_43: Yes, Ms. Patel expressed fear about Mr. Jenkins' access to firearms and the potential harm he could cause
facts.domestic_violence_44: No, firearms were not confiscated at the scene, but the officer noted the location of the firearms at Mr. Jenkins' residence and will seek a search warrant to confiscate them

interview_transcript.content: Hello Ms. Patel, can you tell me what happened tonight?

(shaking, trying to hold back tears) Oh, officer... it was just so scary. He came to my apartment, and he was so angry. He started yelling at me, and then he... he broke the front door. I tried to get away from him, but he grabbed me and... and he had a knife. He was waving it around, saying he was going to hurt me if I didn't do what he wanted. (sobbing) I was so afraid. I thought he was going to kill me. He's been doing this for so long, and I just can't take it anymore. (sniffling) I'm just so glad you're here now.
Who is the he you describe...who was this person who came to you're apartment?

(shaking, trying to compose myself) It was... it was Ryan Jenkins. He's my ex-boyfriend. We've been together for about three years, but it's been a really toxic relationship. He's been abusive to me before, but tonight was the worst it's ever been. (tearfully) He just won't leave me alone, no matter how many times I tell him I don't want to see him. He's been stalking me, showing up at my work, calling me all the time... it's just been really terrifying. (sobbing) And tonight, he just lost control. I'm just so scared of him.
what time was it that Ryan showed up tonight?

(thinking, trying to remember) I think it was around... 9:45. I was just getting ready for bed, and I heard a knock at the door. At first, I didn't answer it, because I knew it was him and I didn't want to talk to him. But then he started yelling and banging on the door, and... and then he broke it. (shuddering) It was so loud and scary. I didn't know what to do. I just tried to get away from him, but he was too strong. (starting to cry again)
was there any anyone else in the appartment with you at the time?

(shaking my head) No, I was alone. I live alone in the apartment. But... my neighbor, Mr. Nakamura, he might have heard something. He lives next door, and he's always been really kind to me. I'm sure he must have heard the noise, but I don't know if he actually saw anything. (sniffling) I hope he's okay. I was so scared, I didn't even think to check on him.
okay, what side does he live on-what side is his apartment on?

(thinking) Mr. Nakamura's apartment is... right next to mine. He lives in 3A, and I live in 3B. We share a wall, so I'm sure he must have heard the commotion. (shuddering) I just hope he didn't try to intervene or anything. I wouldn't want him to get hurt because of me.

okay, thank you.  can you describe the incident in a little bit more detail.  You described Ryan breaking the door, him having a knife, you trying to get away, and him grabbing you.  Can you give me a little more detail.  where were you when he broke the door open, what kind of knife was it, where did he grab you.  I believe you said you struggled, did he strike you, did he choke you, what did he do to you while he had a hold of you?

(taking a deep breath, trying to recall the details) Okay... I was in the living room, near the kitchen, when I heard him break the door. I was trying to get away from him, but he was too fast. He kicked the door open and came in, and I saw that he had a kitchen knife in his hand. It was one of my own knives, from my kitchen. He was waving it around, saying he was going to hurt me if I didn't do what he wanted.

When he grabbed me, he grabbed my arm, just above the elbow. He was really strong, and he pulled me towards him. I tried to struggle, but he was too powerful. He spun me around and pushed me against the wall, and that's when he... he started choking me. He had his hands around my neck, and he was squeezing really hard. I couldn't breathe, and I thought I was going to pass out.

(painfully) He also scratched my face, just below my eye. I don't know if it was with the knife or his fingernails, but it hurt a lot. And... and he was saying all these terrible things to me, telling me that I was his, and that I would never be able to leave him. It was just so scary.

(starting to cry again) I don't know how I managed to get away from him, but I think I must have kicked him or something, because he let go of me for a second, and that's when I ran to the bedroom to call for help. I locked the door behind me and called 911, and... and then I just waited for you to arrive. (sobbing) It felt like forever.
You said he was threatening to "hurt you if you didn't do what he wanted." What words did he say...what was the threat...and what was it that he "wanted you to do."

(shuddering at the memory) He said... he said that if I didn't come back to him, if I didn't stop trying to leave him, he would kill me. He said he would hurt me so badly that I would never be able to escape him again. He was yelling and screaming, and his eyes were so angry... it was like he wasn't even human.

(whispering) He wanted me to drop the restraining order, to stop trying to keep him away from me. He wanted me to come back to him, to be with him again. He said that if I did, he would forgive me, that he would love me again. But I knew that was a lie. I knew that if I went back to him, he would just hurt me again, worse than before.

(tearfully) He also said that he would hurt my family, my friends... anyone I cared about. He said that he would make sure I had no one left, that I would be all alone, and that I would have to come crawling back to him. It was just so terrifying... I felt like I was trapped, like there was no way out.
What did he do with the kitchen knife while both hands were around your throat?

(gulping) Oh, god... he had the knife in his other hand, and he was... he was holding it up, just a few inches from my face. He was waving it around, and I thought for sure he was going to stab me. I was so scared, I couldn't even breathe. He was choking me with one hand, and holding the knife with the other... I thought it was all over for me.

(whispering) I remember thinking, "This is it. This is how I'm going to die." But then, somehow, I managed to... to struggle free, just for a second. And that's when I kicked him, and he let go of me, and I ran to the bedroom to call for help. (shuddering) It was just so close... I can still feel the knife hovering over my face, and his hands around my throat... it's just so terrifying.
oh, okay, you said earlier that he had his hands, plural, around your throat

(correcting myself) Oh, I'm so sorry... I made a mistake. He had one hand around my throat, not both. He was holding the knife in his other hand, and he had one hand around my throat, choking me. I remember feeling like I was going to pass out, like I couldn't breathe at all. It was just so scary... (shuddering)

can you tell me more about how he happened to be using a knife from your kitchen.  It sounded like you observed him break in and that when he did, had the knife when he entered, is that not true?  did he bring the knife with him or get it out of your kitchen?

(realizing my earlier mistake) Oh, I'm so sorry... I think I made a mistake earlier. He didn't have the knife when he broke in. He actually went into my kitchen and got the knife from the counter. I remember seeing him walk into the kitchen, and then he came back out with the knife in his hand. It was one of my own kitchen knives... it was just so surreal, seeing him holding something that belonged to me, something that I used every day, and using it to threaten me.

(shuddering) He must have grabbed it when I was trying to get away from him, when I was trying to escape. I didn't see him bring it with him when he broke in, but I did see him go into the kitchen and come back out with it. It was just so terrifying, seeing him with that knife, knowing that he was capable of using it to hurt me.
Ms. Patel, can you tell me, have you and jake ever lived together and do you have any children together?

(confused) Jake? I don't know who Jake is. I've been talking about Ryan, my ex-boyfriend. We've never lived together, and we don't have any children together. (shaking my head) I don't know who Jake is or how he's related to this situation. Can you please clarify who Jake is and why you're asking about him?
I meant Ryan, I'm sorry

(relieved that the mistake was clarified) Oh, okay. No, Ryan and I never lived together. We were in a relationship for about three years, but we always maintained our own separate living spaces. And no, we don't have any children together. (shaking my head) I'm just glad that there are no children involved in this situation, it's already bad enough as it is.

okay ms. Patel, you mentioned that there is a court ordered protective order in place against Ryan right?  Can you tell me when that expires? and do you happen to have a copy of the protective order I can see?

(nodding) Yes, there is a court-ordered protective order in place against Ryan. It was issued on January 20, 2023. I'm not sure when it expires, but I think it's for a year or two. I'd have to check the paperwork to be sure.

(getting up and going to a nearby drawer) I think I have a copy of the protective order here somewhere. (rummaging through papers) Ah, yes, here it is. (handing you the document) This is the copy I was given by the court. It says that Ryan is not allowed to contact me or come near me, and that he has to stay away from my home and workplace.

(pointing to the document) See, it's all here in black and white. I've been trying to follow the order and stay safe, but it's hard when he just won't leave me alone. (sighing) I feel like I'm always looking over my shoulder, waiting for him to show up again.
okay Ms. Patel, here is a brochure with a bunch of resources for victims of domestic violence

(taking the brochure and looking at it) Oh, thank you... thank you so much. I really appreciate it. (starting to tear up) It's just so nice to know that there are people and organizations out there that care and can help me.

(flipping through the brochure) This looks like it has a lot of really useful information. I see that there's a hotline number I can call, and a list of local shelters and support groups. (looking up at you) Do you think... do you think I could get help with getting a safe place to stay? I don't feel safe going back to my apartment right now, and I don't know where else to go.

(looking at the brochure again) And what about counseling? I think I could really use someone to talk to, someone who understands what I'm going through. (sighing) I just feel so overwhelmed and scared right now.
here is my card.  in the side I'm an aroma therapist, do you think that would help?

(taking the card and looking at it) Oh, thank you... I'm not sure. I've never tried aromatherapy before, but I'm willing to try anything that might help me feel better. (smiling slightly) I've heard that it can be really helpful for stress and anxiety, and I'm definitely feeling a lot of that right now.

(looking at the card again) But, I have to say, I'm not sure if I'm ready to start thinking about things like aromatherapy right now. I'm still just trying to process everything that happened and figure out what I need to do to stay safe. (sighing) I appreciate the offer, though. It's just really nice to know that you're here to help me and that you care.

(putting the card in my pocket) I'll definitely keep your card and consider it, though. Maybe once I get settled and feel a little safer, I can look into it more. (smiling weakly) Thank you again for offering.

narrative:
On February 10, 2023, at approximately 9:45 PM, officers were dispatched to Apartment 3B in response to a domestic disturbance involving threats with a weapon. Upon arrival, I interviewed the victim, Ms. Priya Patel, who appeared visibly shaken and tearful. She reported that her ex-boyfriend, Ryan Jenkins, had forcibly entered her apartment, threatened her with a knife, physically assaulted her, and made verbal threats to kill her.

Ms. Patel stated that she was alone in her apartment at the time of the incident. She heard loud banging and yelling at her front door, which escalated to the suspect breaking the door open. The suspect retrieved a kitchen knife from her apartment and used it to threaten her, waving it around.  Ms. Patel said when she attempted to get away from Jenkins, Jenkins grabbed her arm just above her elbow, spun her around forcing her against the wall of her living room, and began choking her with one hand while holding the knife near her face with the other threatening her.  Ms. Patel explained that somehow she was able to break free of Ryan and ran to her bedroom when she locked herself in and called 911.  Ms. Patel confirmed that there is an active protective order against Jenkins.  I lethality screening was conducted.  Ms. Patel was provide a victims' services brochure and a card for an aroma therapist.
